message:
perf(db): add criterion benchmark for inbox-fetch hot path

Introduce a dedicated criterion benchmark for
`fetch_inbox_rows_from_conn`, which is the read-side hot path
every agent hits at session start and on every `fetch_inbox`
call.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/Cargo.toml
- New `[[bench]]` entry:
    name = "inbox_bench"
    harness = false
  No `required-features` — the benchmark runs against the default
  feature set (in-memory sqlite via `DbConn::open_memory`), so
  `cargo bench -p mcp-agent-mail-db --bench inbox_bench` works
  out of the box.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/benches/inbox_bench.rs (new)
- Seeds a fresh in-memory DB with 10K messages distributed across
  synthetic agents, applies
  `schema::PRAGMA_DB_INIT_SQL`, and measures
  `fetch_inbox_rows_from_conn` at a spread of limits via criterion's
  `BenchmarkId` fan-out so results report per-limit throughput.
- `block_on` helper spins up a fresh asupersync current-thread
  runtime per seeding phase to match how callers invoke the async
  fetch surface without pulling in a full scheduler.
- `seeded_conn(n_messages)` returns
  `(DbConn, project_id, Vec<agent_id>)` so the benchmark closure
  can rotate the `for_agent` arg across agents and avoid always
  hitting the same cache line.
- `black_box`es the result to keep LLVM from eliding the query.

Immediate motivation: we keep seeing inbox-fetch show up on hot
paths in tracing spans but have no way to bisect which SQL
change helped or regressed it; the bench gives us a fixed-input
yardstick.

files.patch:
@@ -107,5 +107,9 @@ name = "two_tier_bench"
 harness = false
 required-features = ["hybrid"]
 
+[[bench]]
+name = "inbox_bench"
+harness = false
+
 [lints]
 workspace = true
